accessNeeds.notes: No specific access need reported. Uses an Android mobile device in typical on-the-go conditions.

requiredAssertions: Layouts remain usable on an Android mobile viewport.
Touch interactions work reliably on primary controls and navigation.
Forms and inputs are usable with mobile virtual keyboards.
Content remains understandable when the viewport changes or the browser UI shifts.
Essential actions remain available during typical mobile interruptions or constrained conditions.
antiPatterns: Assuming desktop-only layout widths or interaction patterns.
Using controls that depend on hover or precise pointer movement.
Allowing virtual keyboards to obscure critical fields or actions.
Using fixed elements that consume excessive mobile screen space.
Creating brittle flows that fail when the viewport changes during interaction.

accessNeeds.notes: No limiting health conditions or special access needs reported.

requiredAssertions: Clickable controls respond to pointer interaction.
Hover and pointer-discoverable affordances are visible when relevant.
Interactive elements have a clear visual affordance before interaction.
Pointer interaction does not depend on overly precise hit areas.
antiPatterns: Relying on hover alone to reveal essential content or actions.
Using tiny click targets that are difficult to activate reliably.
Hiding important actions until pointer hover with no alternative access path.
Using non-semantic clickable elements without appropriate roles and names.

accessNeeds.notes: No limiting health conditions or special access needs reported, but currently relies on keyboard for all interaction.

requiredAssertions: All interactive controls are reachable using only the keyboard.
Focus order follows the visual and logical reading order.
Focused elements have a visible focus indicator.
Buttons, links, menus, and dialogs are operable with Enter and Space where appropriate.
antiPatterns: Removing the default focus outline without a replacement.
Creating keyboard traps in dialogs, menus, or custom widgets.
Using div or span elements as controls without keyboard support.
Reordering focus with positive tabindex values.
Revealing essential functionality only on hover.
